channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Nevada lawmakers target 'resort fee'-style rent practices, age for gun ownership
item.description: Hidden fees are so out-of-hand that it now costs money just to pay the rent. And it's a good bet that tenants aren't told that until they have signed the lease.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Acting head of Social Security quits after clash with DOGE over data: Report
item.description: The acting head of the Social Security Administration reportedly quit her job over the weekend after butting heads with DOGE over access to sensitive government records.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Social Security head steps down over DOGE access of recipient information: AP sources
item.description: The Social Security Administration’s acting commissioner has stepped down from her role at the agency over Department of Government Efficiency requests to access Social Security recipient information.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: NY Gov. Hochul to meet with 'key leaders' to discuss 'path forward' amid Eric Adams turmoil
item.description: NYC Comptroller Brad Lander wants Mayor Eric Adams to provide a plan this month on how he will govern the city amid turmoil and drama at City Hall
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former Vice President Kamala Harris to receive NAACP's Chairman's Award
item.description: Former Vice President Kamala Harris will receive the NAACP's Chairman's Award during the civil rights organization’s Image Awards ceremony this weekend. The Chairman's Award honors individuals who excel in public service and leverage their unique platforms to ignite and drive meaningful change.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Nevada Democrat reintroduces 'fake electors' bill following governor's veto
item.description: Nevada lawmakers will once again debate a bill to penalize a person who creates a false slate of presidential electors -- except this time with lesser penalties.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: DOGE says it found nearly untraceable budget line item responsible for $4.7T in payments
item.description: DOGE identified a nearly untraceable code linking payments to the U.S. Treasury to a budget line item, accounting for nearly $4.7 trillion in payments.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House, Senate standoff over Trump agenda escalates
item.description: The showdown between House and Senate Republicans is escalating as the two groups barrel ahead with their contrasting strategies to pass President Trump’s legislative agenda — with the upper chamber expected to move this week.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Elon Musk’s DOGE seeks access to taxpayer data at IRS: AP sources
item.description: If successful, Musk and his group would have access to millions of tightly controlled files that include taxpayer information, bank records and other sensitive records.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Boston police commissioner doubles down on ICE resistance: 'We don’t enforce' detainers
item.description: Democrat-appointed Boston Police Commissioner Michael Cox is echoing Mayor Michelle Wu's pledged resistance to the Trump administration's deportations, saying on a local news station on Sunday that "we don’t enforce" civil immigration detainers filed by ICE.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fox News Politics Newletter: Duffy Calls Out Clinton
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump administration, exclusive interviews and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge to rule swiftly on effort to block DOGE from assessing data and firing federal employees
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Monday questioned the authority of billionaire Elon Musk and his Department of Government Efficiency but was skeptical of a request to block DOGE from accessing sensitive data and firing employees at half a dozen federal agencies. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Energy secretary blasts 'sinister' zero carbon emissions goal
item.description: “Net zero 2050 is a sinister goal, it’s a terrible goal,” Wright said to applause at the Alliance for Responsible Citizenship forum.
item.dc:creator: Zack Budryk, NewsNation

item.title: Top Democrats question IRS on DOGE access to taxpayer data
item.description: Top Democrats in Congress want to know what Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) plans to do at the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) after multiple outlets reported the advisory unit sought access to sensitive IRS systems. Senate Finance Committee members Ron Wyden (D-Ore.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, NewsNation

item.title: Conservative group slaps top federal agency with lawsuit over Biden-era handling of illegal immigrants
item.description: Center to Advance Security in America is suing the Department of Homeland Security for data related to the release of criminal illegal immigrants into the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump suggests no laws violated if he ‘saves’ country
item.description: Trump doubled down on the Napoleon reference on Sunday, reposting an image of the French emperor riding into battle with the text "He who saves his country violates no law" overlaid.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: NAACP guide urges Black Americans to avoid companies that dumped DEI
item.description: The advisory also lists companies that have recommitted to DEI in recent weeks – such as Delta Airlines, Apple and Ben & Jerry’s.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, NewsNation

item.title: RFK Jr. rescinds Indian Health Service layoffs
item.description: The last-minute decision to rescind the layoff order for IHS came as health care workers waited for bad news.
item.dc:creator: MPR News

item.title: Vivek Ramaswamy set to launch expected 2026 run for Ohio governor
item.description: The biotech entrepreneur would join Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost in the race to succeed term-limited Gov. Mike DeWine in the Buckeye State.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: FAA firings begin weeks after deadly crash near DC
item.description: The Trump administration has begun firing hundreds of employees at the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) just weeks after a deadly crash outside of Washington, D.C., put a spotlight on the agency.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Trump nominates Ed Martin, an ally who defended Jan. 6 rioters, to stay as top DC federal prosecutor
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Conservative activist Ed Martin, who echoed Donald Trump's baseless claims of a stolen 2020 election and defended Jan. 6, 2021, U.S. Capitol rioters, was nominated by the president on Monday to be the top federal prosecutor in Washington on a permanent basis.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Washington Post backs out of 'Fire Elon Musk' ad order
item.description: The Washington Post this week backed out of a “Fire Elon Musk” advertising order that was to run as a wrap on some of its Tuesday editions, according to the advocacy group Common Cause.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: To these Black retirees, the federal civil service now under attack was a path to the middle class
item.description: The federal civil service that President Donald Trump and Elon Musk are dismantling has been a way for people of color, in particular Black Americans, to get a toehold into the middle class for decades, as other occupations were closed off because of racial discrimination or patronage.
item.dc:creator: Gary Fields, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Democrats grapple for response on education as GOP charges ahead
item.description: The Democratic Party is having to take a hard look at its education strategy as Republicans move aggressively on the issue with satisfaction in public education at its lowest point in decades.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How a South Texas community college embraced apprenticeships to ease a growing nursing shortage
item.description: South Texas College in McAllen launched one of the first registered nursing apprenticeships in the country as area hospitals expect the need for nurses to increase.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Sneha Dey, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump administration tries to bring back fired nuclear weapons workers, reversing DOGE cuts
item.description: he Trump administration has halted the firings of hundreds of federal employees who were tasked with working on the nation's nuclear weapons programs, in an about-face that has left workers confused and experts cautioning that DOGE's blind cost cutting will put communities at risk.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration wants the Supreme Court to let the firing of whistleblower agency head proceed
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration wants the Supreme Court to permit the firing of the head of the federal agency dedicated to protecting whistleblowers, according to documents obtained Sunday that would mark the first appeal to the justices since President Donald Trump took office.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democratic governors diverge on how to handle Trump
item.description: Democratic governors are diverging in their approaches to President Trump amid mounting speculation that some of them are gunning for the White House in 2028. Some are digging their heels into resistance efforts, as in Illinois, where Gov.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Elon Musk shared Mars plan over drinks, Star Trek convo: Entrepreneur
item.description: "I realized he was dead serious. Elon Musk doesn't just dream, he makes things happen," said entrepreneur and academic Vivek Wadhwa.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Zouves, NewsNation

item.title: Sheryl Crow sells Tesla to donate funds to embattled NPR
item.description: “Money donated to NPR, which is under threat by President Musk, in hopes that the truth will continue to find its way to those willing to know the truth,” the country artist wrote.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, NewsNation

item.title: NYC public advocate questions Mayor Adams’ ability to govern
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York City Public Advocate Jumaane Williams says Mayor Eric Adams can no longer govern the city “the way it needs to be governed.” The criticism came Friday after New York Lt. Gov.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Trump’s buyout offer accepted by about 77,000 federal workers, White House says
item.description: The number amounts to less than 4% of the federal workforce, short of the White House's goal of 5% to 10%.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, Fox 5

item.title: CA, 18 other states' AGs celebrate injunction against Trump's citizenship order
item.description: LOS ANGELES (KTLA)—California Attorney General Rob Bonta and 18 other state attorneys general have issued a statement after another judge blocked President Donald Trump's executive order seeking to terminate birthright citizenship.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Trump hired Musk as a ‘special government employee.’ Here’s what that means
item.description: Special government employees, or SGEs, are typically hired on a temporary basis as experts or consultants or serve as members of federal advisory committees.
item.dc:creator: Joe Hernandez, MPR News

item.title: Durbin calls for investigation into Patel's role in FBI firings
item.description: Sen. Dick Durbin (D-Ill.) is asking the Department of Justice’s (DOJ) inspector general to investigate “highly credible information” that Kash Patel, President Trump’s pick to head the FBI, is already directing a “purge” of the bureau before he’s been confirmed by the Senate.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5
